title: helianthus occidentalis var. illinoensis
intro: Helianthus occidentalis var. illinoensis, also known as Illinois prairie coneflower or prairie coneflower, is a perennial plant native to the prairies of the Midwestern United States. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Helianthus occidentalis var. illinoensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies.
